items.title: Build a first proof
items.summary: Compile a complete CT1 residual, capability, executor, query, and route walkthrough.

items.title: Choose a CT
items.summary: Match a mathematical split to all computed outcomes of CT1–CT17.

items.title: Implement a graph theorem
items.summary: Register finite graph semantics and use Graph-owned CT adapters.

items.title: Register a PDE model
items.summary: Define an atlas, represented equation, coordinates, and analytic boundaries.

items.title: Find an API
items.summary: Search compiled declarations, exact types, modules, and source ranges.

items.title: Read a full application
items.summary: Follow source-derived nodes and predecessor edges in Erdős Problem 64.

stages.label: Register
stages.detail: Author supplies the problem, target, and irreducible semantics.

stages.label: Execute
stages.detail: A CT computes a typed alternative from the literal predecessor.

stages.label: Accumulate
stages.detail: Core extends the proof ledger without copying inherited facts.

stages.label: Route
stages.detail: A typed transition discovers input and invokes the target executor.

stages.label: Close
stages.detail: A terminal closes only when its computed evidence matches the target.

home.title: Framework architecture
home.description: Applications register semantics; domain adapters prepare CT inputs; Core retains the literal ledger through execution, routing, and closure.

nodes.label: Application
nodes.summary: Problem, target, and irreducible theorem inputs

nodes.label: Graph adapter
nodes.summary: Finite graph semantics and CT constructors

nodes.label: PDE adapter
nodes.summary: Represented analytic semantics and CT constructors

nodes.label: Core ledger
nodes.summary: Stable residual and accumulated typed evidence

home.title: Responsibility boundary
rows: Input size and local bound proof

home.title: What verification means
home.body: Published declarations come from a Lean-generated catalog. Source ranges are hash-verified and content-bound to the published snapshot. Imported analytic or graph facts remain named at their explicit theorem boundary; the site does not turn a source record into a proof claim.

start.title: A complete runnable quickstart
start.caption: From the repository root: cd hypostructure && lake env lean --stdin < ../web/content/snippets/quickstart.lean

start.title: What the route section demonstrates
start.body: The application owns a typed target specification, capability, semantic profile, and one public route function. The framework owns discovery, target execution, provenance, and the single extension of the exact CT1 predecessor stage.

items.title: CT1.execute
items.summary: Computes target realization or exact avoidance and retains the literal predecessor.

items.title: Core.Residual.Query.residual
items.summary: Reads the stable root residual from the accumulated CT and routed stages.

items.title: Routes.Accumulated.register / advance
items.summary: Registers an application-owned semantic profile and executes its target capability on the complete source stage.

start.title: End-to-end recipe
items: Define `Core.Problem` and a separate target predicate.
Choose the stable residual and initialize `Core.Residual.Ledger` once.
Select a CT by matching all of its complementary outcomes to the mathematical split.
Provide `Spec`, the minimal `Capability`, finite schedules, and decision procedures.
Run the public executor on the literal predecessor and query inherited facts.
Advance only through a typed `Core.Routing.Transition` or close from computed terminal evidence.
Add fixtures for every outcome, exact predecessor preservation, work bounds, and axiom audits.

start.title: How to read an executor
rows: The target or another closing condition is certified
Consume the certificate through Core closure
The complementary alternative is exhaustive and typed
Pass that exact stage to its registered consumer
The observable decision path selected by execution
Use for audit; never author it
Compare with the capability's polynomial bound

start.title: Reject these implementation shapes
items: Constructing a CT terminal in application code
Replacing the predecessor with a convenient local record
Copying earlier theorems instead of querying the ledger
Treating a route name as an executable transition
Sending raw catalogs to React for joins or classification

core.title: What Hypostructure automates
rows: Ambient type, baseline, branch state, target meaning
Typed contexts and stable residual access
Semantic candidates, labels, responses, or budget predicates
Residual-owned schedules, decision procedures, size measure, and work-bound proof
A Spec, Capability, and literal predecessor
Public executor call and proof-facing helper syntax
Outcome, terminal, exact trace, checks, ledger extension, and predecessor preservation
A typed profile only where semantic discovery exists
Enabled/disabled discovery, target input, provenance, target execution, and routed extension
Irreducible domain theorem and proofs connecting semantics to the target
Transport, bookkeeping, complementary residual, and framework closure

items.title: Problem registration
items.summary: Ambient objects, baseline contexts, branch state, and the target-independent problem boundary.

items.title: Residual ledger
items.summary: One stable residual with dependent extensions that retain each literal predecessor.

items.title: Residual query
items.summary: Typed reads of the stable root or inherited facts without copying them into a new record.

items.title: Residual decision
items.summary: Framework-owned complementary branching and branch-indexed evidence.

items.title: Residual focus
items.summary: Active and inactive focus states for predecessor-owned local selections.

items.title: Execution substrate
items.summary: Typed specifications, deterministic capabilities, exact work, and generated stage outputs.

items.title: Routing
items.summary: Semantic discovery, target input construction, provenance, and complete-ledger transitions.

items.title: Closure
items.summary: Target-bearing terminals and the final proof boundary.

core.title: Core capability map
rows: Ambient objects, baseline contexts, optional progress
Stable carrier, dependent stages, queries, decisions, focus, joins
Specifications, capabilities, reference results, exact checks
Typed edges, discovery, provenance, complete-ledger transition
Target terminals and propagated closed-class ledgers

core.title: Two ledgers, two jobs
rows: Dependent propositions and stages about one residual
Mathematical classes already proved null or closed

core.title: Executable route criterion
core.body: An edge becomes executable only with a typed profile, semantic discovery, target-input construction, a real target capability, provenance, and a public advance operation that preserves the complete predecessor ledger.

items.title: FiniteObject
items.summary: A Mathlib simple graph plus the deterministic vertex schedule and decidable adjacency needed by finite executors.

items.title: Finite observables
items.summary: Derived vertex, edge, degree, and neighbourhood views without duplicate application data.

items.title: Boundary pieces
items.summary: Labelled finite interfaces, locally owned pieces, and outside contexts.

items.title: Exact gluing
items.summary: Literal boundary union and the Core assembly adapter with reconstruction isomorphism.

items.title: Response systems
items.summary: Exact same-boundary response coordinates, comparison, and target-complete equivalence.

items.title: Proper boundaried atoms
items.summary: Connected local pieces, boundary profiles, focused execution, and work accounting.

items.title: Atom response coordinates
items.summary: Response coordinates and quotients represented on one literal boundaried atom.

items.title: Rooted-return algebra
items.summary: The exact dictionary between an edge-rooted return and the restored simple cycle.

items.title: Graph target API
items.summary: Cycle certificates, external target interfaces, and isomorphism-invariant transport.

items.title: Graph basics fixture
items.summary: Compiled K4, induced-support, deletion, isomorphism, and cycle-target examples.

items.title: Rooted-return fixture
items.summary: A compiled K4 return certificate and its public cycle decoding theorems.

graph.title: Implement a graph problem
items: Pack the finite graph and its deterministic vertex/edge schedules.
Register the baseline and branch state with `Graph.problem`; keep the target external.
Use graph isomorphism as the semantic equivalence and prove target invariance.
Add only the locality, deletion, response, progress, boundary, or budget capabilities the selected CT needs.
Construct the Graph CT adapter from residual-owned graph data and run its Core-compatible executor.
Test both graph-specific outcomes and preservation of the root residual.

stages.label: Finite object
stages.detail: Graph plus enumerations and decidable adjacency

stages.label: CT adapter
stages.detail: Generic finite decision over graph semantics

stages.label: Accumulated stage
stages.detail: Typed output on the original proof ledger

graph.title: Graph failure modes
items: Embedding theorem-specific constants in reusable Graph records
Using literal equality where isomorphism is the declared semantics
Manually relabelling or gluing in an application node
Selecting a root, edge, or support twice after a CT already stored it

pde.title: Compile a represented PDE model
pde.caption: From the repository root: cd hypostructure && lake env lean --stdin < ../web/content/snippets/pde_quickstart.lean

items.title: LocalModel
items.summary: The mandatory registration: Core problem, local atlas, and represented equation only.

items.title: FastTrack.Signature
items.summary: The minimal target-facing row: representation semantics, observables, invariance, and target interface.

items.title: GeneratorForm
items.summary: Optional generator, form topology, closure law, decomposition, positivity, and sector bound.

items.title: Represented quotient and defect
items.summary: Projection/lift semantics and the framework-computed intertwining defect with an exhaustive geometry split.

items.title: Coordinate API
items.summary: Restriction, recentering, rescaling, and framework-owned coordinate paths.

items.title: Local-tail assembly
items.summary: Exact additive reconstruction exposed through Core atom/context assembly.

items.title: Observable invariance
items.summary: Optional representation invariance for public observable families.

items.title: External target interface
items.summary: Observable characterization of a theorem target and derived Core target transport.

items.title: Window charging adapter
items.summary: PDE-owned preparation for CT4 deterministic charging over analytic windows.

items.title: Finite classification adapter
items.summary: PDE-owned preparation for CT10 observable-signature classification.

items.title: Target thickening adapter
items.summary: PDE-owned preparation for CT17 scale, shell, and modulation arithmetic.

items.title: Finite PDE example
items.summary: A compiled scalar model with an explicit atlas and represented equation.

items.title: Coordinate-path example
items.summary: A two-primitive recenter-then-rescale execution with exact formula and work-free proof.

pde.title: PDE capability levels
rows: `FastTrack.Signature`: representation semantics, observables, observable invariance, target interface
When the proof exposes an external target through public observables
Generator/form, quotient and defect, coordinates, local-tail assembly, compact extraction, budgets
Only when the selected argument uses that analytic structure
Only for CT families actually executed by the application

pde.title: Represented coordinate package
pde.description: Center, scale, nested windows, representative, quotient, topology, retained obstruction, and input contract remain explicit and transportable.

pde.title: Implement a represented PDE model
items: Define a Core problem for the global represented field.
Register a `LocalAtlas` with points, windows, restriction, nesting, and core laws.
Register the represented equation and the intended representation equivalence.
Expose observables independently; give a finite subfamily only to CTs that execute a finite scan.
Add coordinate primitives and local-tail reconstruction laws once at the PDE layer.
Register only the fast-track capabilities required by the current row.
Keep every analytic estimate as a named theorem input and exercise the structural executor on a finite fixture.

pde.title: Structural versus analytic responsibility
rows: Compactness theorem at the named boundary

pde.title: PDE failure modes
items: Hiding a compactness theorem inside an opaque capability
Re-estimating a class already carried by the closed-class ledger
Changing gauge or scale without a registered coordinate action
Treating a finite toy execution as proof of an external analytic theorem

examples.title: Recommended reading order
items: Core primitives and residual-ledger fixtures
One CT fixture with both complementary outcomes
Graph basics, deletion, response, and assembly
Finite PDE model, coordinate path, gauge, and local-tail examples
The dependency graph of the Erdős application

examples.title: What an example certifies
examples.body: An example is linked to its exact source modules and declarations. Execution evidence is attached only when a source-level executor and focused fixture are both present.

erdos.title: Official target
erdos.tex: \delta(G)\ge 3\;\Longrightarrow\;\exists k\ge2,\;\exists C\subseteq G,\; C\text{ is a cycle and }|C|=2^k
erdos.description: For every finite graph of minimum degree at least three, find a cycle whose length is a power of two with exponent at least two.

stages.label: Official statement
stages.detail: Mathlib graph proposition and dyadic cycle length

stages.label: Problem
stages.detail: Finite graph baseline and external target

stages.label: Initial residual
stages.detail: One stable graph and baseline proof

stages.label: Node executors
stages.detail: Literal predecessor plus one framework operation

erdos.title: Node evidence shown by the site
rows: Public node definition in the declaration catalog or source
Explicit `#print axioms` endpoints in native source

erdos.title: Two explicit authorities
erdos.body: The immutable paper supplies all 157 nodes and their direct predecessor edges. Native Hypostructure source modules supply implementation evidence; imports never redefine the paper topology.
